question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause cache thrashing and repeated DB queries by sending requests for blocks whose account IDs cycle through values that exceed the LRU cache `MaxSize`, causing every `entityCache.Get(accountId.GetId())` call to miss and trigger a `GetAccountAlias` DB query, saturating the database?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CPU and memory exhaustion by sending many concurrent `/block/transaction` requests with valid block identifiers, since each request calls `FindByIdentifier` and `FindByHashInBlock` (two DB queries) plus `updateOperationAccountAlias`, multiplying resource consumption per request?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the HTTP server to exhaust file descriptors by sending many concurrent requests that are never closed (slow-read attack), since the `http.Server` has a `WriteTimeout` but if it is set to 0 or a very large value from `rosettaConfig.Http.WriteTimeout`, connections accumulate until the OS file descriptor limit is reached?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause cache thrashing and repeated DB queries by sending requests for blocks whose account IDs cycle through values that exceed the LRU cache `MaxSize`, causing every `entityCache.Get(accountId.GetId())` call to miss and trigger a `GetAccountAlias` DB query, saturating the database?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CPU and memory exhaustion by sending many concurrent `/block/transaction` requests with valid block identifiers, since each request calls `FindByIdentifier` and `FindByHashInBlock` (two DB queries) plus `updateOperationAccountAlias`, multiplying resource consumption per request?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the HTTP server to exhaust file descriptors by sending many concurrent requests that are never closed (slow-read attack), since the `http.Server` has a `WriteTimeout` but if it is set to 0 or a very large value from `rosettaConfig.Http.WriteTimeout`, connections accumulate until the OS file descriptor limit is reached?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the HTTP server to exhaust memory by sending requests with very large request bodies that are never fully read, since `ReadTimeout` controls the entire request read time but if set to 0 from `rosettaConfig.Http.ReadTimeout`, the server will buffer the body indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause goroutine leaks by sending requests that trigger long-running DB queries that exceed the `StatementTimeout` but are not cancelled when the client disconnects, since the server context is not propagated to all DB operations, leaving goroutines blocked on DB responses?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NewConstructionAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the `sdkClient.SetNetworkUpdatePeriod(config.Rosetta.NodeRefreshInterval)` background goroutine, where a very short `NodeRefreshInterval` causes the SDK to continuously poll the Hedera network address book, consuming CPU and network resources that starve the main request-handling goroutines?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the server to block all available goroutines by sending many concurrent `/construction/submit` requests with valid signed transactions, since `hiero.TransactionExecute(transaction, c.sdkClient)` is a blocking call with `SetMaxAttempts(1)` that waits for a network response, and if the Hedera network is slow, all goroutines become blocked?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getRandomNodeAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CPU exhaustion by triggering many concurrent calls to `getRandomNodeAccountId()` via `/construction/metadata` requests, since the function iterates over the entire `c.sdkClient.GetNetwork()` map and builds a deduplicated `nodeAccountIds` slice on every call without caching, consuming CPU proportional to the network size?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: ConstructionMetadata()] Can an unprivileged external user cause memory exhaustion by sending a `/construction/metadata` request with a very large `request.Options` map, since `maps.Clone(request.Options)` creates a full copy of the map and all subsequent metadata operations work on this copy, with no limit on the size of the options map?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: tracingResponseWriter.Write()] Can an unprivileged external user cause memory exhaustion by triggering responses with very large bodies (e.g., requesting blocks with many transactions), since `tracingResponseWriter.Write()` stores the entire response body in `w.data []byte` in memory for every request, and concurrent large responses multiply memory consumption?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the server to fail to start or crash on restart by exploiting the `rosettaAsserter.NewServer(types.SupportedOperationTypes, true, []*rTypes.NetworkIdentifier{network}, nil, false, '')` call with a crafted network identifier that causes the asserter to enter an invalid state, making all subsequent request validations fail with panics?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user cause database connection exhaustion by repeatedly polling the `/health/readiness` endpoint, since each readiness check creates a new postgres connection via `postgres.New(postgres.Config{DSN: rosettaConfig.Db.GetDsn()})` to verify connectivity, consuming a DB connection slot per health check invocation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a CPU spike by sending requests that trigger the `fmt.Sprintf('%s:%d', relativeFilepath, frame.Line)` call in `configLogger`'s `CallerPrettyfier` at `log.DebugLevel`, where enabling debug logging causes every request to generate a stack frame lookup and string formatting operation, saturating CPU?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: ConstructionParse()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CPU exhaustion by sending many concurrent `/construction/parse` requests with large valid transaction hex strings, since `unmarshallTransactionFromHexString` performs `hex.DecodeString` and `hiero.TransactionFromBytes` (proto deserialization) for each request, and `c.transactionHandler.Parse` processes the full transaction, consuming CPU proportional to transaction size?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: ConstructionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CPU exhaustion by sending many concurrent `/construction/hash` requests with large hex-encoded transaction strings, since each request performs `hex.DecodeString`, `hiero.TransactionFromBytes`, and `hiero.TransactionGetTransactionHash` without any rate limiting or request size cap?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the server to exhaust memory by sending HTTP requests with very large headers, since the `http.Server` struct does not set `MaxHeaderBytes` explicitly, relying on the Go default of 1MB, and if the default is insufficient or misconfigured, large header payloads can cause memory pressure across many concurrent connections?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newBlockchainOnlineRouter()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a goroutine leak by sending requests that trigger the `serverContext` cancellation path in `NewBlockAPIService`, where the LRU cache background goroutine started by `cache.NewContext(serverContext, ...)` does not properly clean up, leaving orphaned goroutines that accumulate over repeated server restarts?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: ConstructionCombine()] Can an unprivileged external user cause memory exhaustion by sending a `/construction/combine` request where each signature in `request.Signatures` has a very large `PublicKey.Bytes` slice, since `hiero.PublicKeyFromBytes(signature.PublicKey.Bytes)` is called for each signature and may allocate large internal structures before returning an error?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: ConstructionDerive()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CPU exhaustion by sending many concurrent `/construction/derive` requests with large `PublicKey.Bytes` payloads, since `types.NewAccountIdFromPublicKeyBytes(request.PublicKey.Bytes, c.systemShard, c.systemRealm)` processes the bytes without a size check, and concurrent processing of large keys saturates CPU?
